metadata.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) Server Component

components.title: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) Server
components.description: Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) 9 deployed on organisation servers hosting Linux-based workloads (web servers, application servers, containerised workloads, database servers). RHEL 9 is managed via Red Hat Satellite (or equivalent) for patch management, and hardened per CIS Benchmark for RHEL 9 and ASD guidance. SELinux is enforced in targeted policy mode.

props.name: management
props.value: Red Hat Satellite / Ansible Automation Platform / SSH

props.name: security-features
props.value: SELinux (enforcing, targeted policy), firewalld, auditd, AIDE, FIPS mode

protocols.name: ssh
protocols.title: SSH — Primary secure remote management and file transfer (SCP/SFTP)

protocols.name: https
protocols.title: HTTPS — Red Hat Satellite / Ansible Tower management, package download

protocols.name: kerberos
protocols.title: Kerberos — Active Directory authentication (sssd/realmd AD integration)

protocols.name: ldap
protocols.title: LDAP — Active Directory user/group lookups via sssd

protocols.name: ldaps
protocols.title: LDAPS — Secure LDAP for AD integration

protocols.name: syslog
protocols.title: Syslog — Audit and security event forwarding to SIEM (rsyslog)

control-implementations.description: Control implementations for Red Hat Enterprise Linux (RHEL) Server.

implemented-requirements.description: An approved hardening baseline for RHEL 9 is developed and maintained based on the CIS Benchmark for RHEL 9 and ASD hardening guidance for Linux. Key hardening steps: SELinux set to enforcing (targeted policy); unnecessary services and packages removed (yum remove); firewalld enabled with default-deny; auditd configured with the CIS-recommended audit rules; AIDE file integrity monitoring configured and run nightly; FIPS 140-2 mode enabled; all setuid/setgid binaries reviewed; SSH hardened (PasswordAuthentication no, PermitRootLogin no, key-based auth only); and login banner configured. The baseline is enforced via Ansible playbooks and verified by OpenSCAP compliance scanning.

RHEL 9 servers are hardened using ASD Linux hardening guidance and the CIS Benchmark for RHEL 9. Where guidance conflicts, ASD guidance takes precedence. SSH is the sole management protocol (Telnet, rsh, and rlogin are not installed); root login via SSH is disabled; only key-based SSH authentication is permitted; password authentication is disabled for all SSH sessions; and the SSH daemon is configured with approved cipher suites and MACs only (matching ASD-approved cryptographic settings).

Security patches and updates for RHEL 9 packages are applied within one month of release (within 48 hours for critical patches or those with active exploits) via Red Hat Satellite or yum/dnf automated patching. Patch compliance is verified via Red Hat Satellite compliance reporting or OpenSCAP scans run at least fortnightly.

RHEL 9 security events are centrally logged to the SIEM via rsyslog or a log agent. Auditd is configured to capture: authentication events (PAM), privilege escalation (sudo), file system changes to sensitive paths (/etc/passwd, /etc/sudoers, SSH keys), network connection events, and SELinux policy violations. All auditd events are forwarded to the SIEM in near-real time.
